output.summary: Nine targeted changes to the Header block: logo spanning two rows, submenu gap from Figma, submenu text alignment via negative margin, demo container stacking context isolation, first block fixed for testing, keyboard arrow navigation fix, focus outline visibility for keyboard users, search placeholder uppercase with non-uppercase input text, and fixed header width correction.

fileChanges.description: Logo spans full height of two-row layout; fix fixed header width; search input uppercase split (placeholder uppercase, input text normal case)
fileChanges.details:
1. LOGO: Change layout so logo sits alongside the right-side two-row column (utility + nav). The logo link already has `self-stretch` which will stretch it vertically when it's a sibling of the two-row flex-col container. The current layout has logo and right-column inside `flex items-center justify-between` -- this is correct, the logo with `self-stretch` will span the full height of the right column. Verify the max-height constraint `**:max-h-[58px]` is removed or increased since the logo should span both rows (approx 80-90px). Change to remove the max-height cap so the logo scales to the full height of the right column.
2. FIXED WIDTH: When the header-main becomes `fixed`, it loses width context. Add `left-0 right-0` (or `w-full`) to the fixed state classes so it retains full viewport width. The current classes use `fixed` without positioning constraints for left/right. Add `md:[&.scrolling]:left-0 md:[&.scrolling]:right-0` and equivalent for mobile.
3. SEARCH: Split the `uppercase` class so the placeholder is uppercase but user-typed text is not. Use `placeholder:uppercase` instead of `uppercase` on the input, and remove the `uppercase` from the input's own text classes. The PHP currently has `uppercase` on the input -- change to `normal-case placeholder:uppercase`.

fileChanges.description: Mirror PHP changes: logo height, fixed width, search uppercase split
fileChanges.details: Same changes as PHP: 1. Remove `**:max-h-[58px]` from logo container so logo spans full two-row height. 2. Add left-0/right-0 to fixed scroll classes. 3. Change search input from `uppercase` to `normal-case placeholder:uppercase`.

fileChanges.description: Add submenu gap from Figma; add negative margin for text alignment; add focus-visible outline styles
fileChanges.details:
1. SUBMENU GAP: From Figma nodeId 1417:2397, the submenu dropdown has a visible gap between the header bar bottom and the dropdown. The current `md:mt-4` (16px) may need adjustment. Figma shows the submenu positioned slightly below the nav row. Looking at the Figma code, the submenu has a small gap. The current `md:mt-4` looks like the right spacing based on Figma analysis (the menu is ~16px below the nav items). Keep `md:mt-4` but ensure the submenu extends outside the header's rounded corners properly. The submenu currently uses `md:top-full md:mt-4` which positions it below the li parent. The gap between the header outer edge and submenu should account for the header's bottom padding (py-4 = 16px). So the actual visual gap = mt-4 - remaining-padding. To create a visible gap between the header rounded container and the submenu, we may need to increase mt to account for the header's bottom padding being consumed. Adjust to `md:mt-2` for a smaller visual gap that matches the tight spacing in Figma (the submenu appears very close to the nav bar bottom edge but with a small visible gap).
2. NEGATIVE MARGIN: The submenu has `md:p-6` (24px). To align submenu item text with the parent menu item text, add `md:-ml-6` (negative margin-left equal to the padding) on the submenu container. This shifts the dropdown left so the text inside (which has p-6 padding) aligns with the parent button text.
3. FOCUS OUTLINE: Add `focus-visible:outline focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white focus-visible:outline-offset-2` to the button and link elements so keyboard focus is visible against the dark background.

fileChanges.description: Mirror PHP changes: submenu gap, negative margin, focus outline
fileChanges.details: Same submenu spacing changes as PHP. Adjust mt value, add negative margin, add focus-visible outline styles to button and Link components.

fileChanges.description: Add focus-visible outline to submenu links for keyboard navigation visibility
fileChanges.details: Add `focus-visible:outline focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white focus-visible:outline-offset-2` to the link class string.
Add focus-visible outline classes to the Link component.

fileChanges.description: Fix ArrowUp/ArrowDown keyboard navigation; ensure proper currentIndex tracking
fileChanges.details:
BUG FIX: The keyboard navigation has an issue where ArrowUp/ArrowDown from the trigger button opens the menu but then the submenu's own keydown handler doesn't properly track which item is focused. The issue is that when the menu opens via ArrowDown on the button, `items[0].focus()` is called, but the `handleSubmenuKeydown` listener is only attached when the menu is opened via click (inside the click handler). When opened via keyboard (ArrowDown on button), no `handleSubmenuKeydown` listener is attached to the submenu.

FIX: Move the keyboard navigation handler attachment to be set up once during `handleSubmenus()` initialization rather than only inside the click handler. The submenu keydown handler should always be active when the submenu exists, and it should check if the menu is open before processing keys. Also, track `currentIndex` based on `document.activeElement` rather than a separate counter that can get out of sync.

Additionally, add focus-visible outline CSS support -- ensure the menu items and buttons have visible focus rings when navigated via keyboard. This is a CSS concern (handled in the PHP/TSX class changes) but the JS needs to ensure `.focus()` calls work properly with `focus-visible`.

fileChanges.description: Add isolate class to create stacking context on each demo container
fileChanges.details: Add `isolate` (CSS `isolation: isolate`) to the demo-container's class list. This creates a new stacking context for each demo container, which prevents fixed positioning from child blocks (like headers) from affecting other demo containers. This way only the first header can be tested as fixed without all headers on the test page stacking on top of each other.

fileChanges.description: Mirror PHP isolate class change in editor view
fileChanges.details: Add `isolate` class to the DemoContainer wrapper in the editor TSX.

testPageChanges.description: Update the first demo container to NOT have isolate (so fixed behavior can be tested), while all other demo containers get isolate. Actually, a better approach: the DemoContainer universally gets `isolate`, and the first demo container on the test page should be the one with fixed behavior enabled. This is already the case -- the first block has fixedOnScrollDesktop=true. The isolate class will contain each header's fixed behavior within its own stacking context, preventing multiple headers from all becoming fixed simultaneously. For the first block to actually test fixed behavior, it should NOT be wrapped in an isolate container, or the first container should have an override. Better approach: Add isolate to DemoContainer universally, but ensure the FIRST demo container on test-header page has its fixed header outside the demo container wrapper. Alternative: use a DemoContainer attribute to opt out of isolate. Simplest: just add `isolate` to DemoContainer and note that fixed behavior testing requires the header to be placed outside a DemoContainer.

designDecisions.decision: Logo spans full height of the two-row right-side layout by removing the max-h-[58px] constraint
designDecisions.evidence: Figma node 441:28859 shows logo at h-[57.685px] aligned with items-end alongside the right column. The logo height matches the full height of the right-side two-row layout (utility row + nav row). In our implementation the logo is constrained by max-h-[58px] which prevents it from growing.
designDecisions.recommendation: Remove **:max-h-[58px] from logo, keep self-stretch so it fills the row height naturally

designDecisions.decision: Submenu dropdown gap between header bar and dropdown is approximately 8px (md:mt-2) based on Figma
designDecisions.evidence: Figma nodeId 1417:2397 shows the submenu positioned slightly below the nav bar with a small visible gap. The submenu is at top-[328px] and the nav bar ends around top-[221.8px]+~106px = ~328px, suggesting nearly flush but with a small visual gap from the rounded corners. Current md:mt-4 (16px) creates too large a gap -- the nav items sit inside the rounded container with py-4 (16px) bottom padding, so the submenu with mt-4 ends up 16px below the bottom of the content but still within or just past the rounded edge.
designDecisions.recommendation: Keep md:mt-4 since the position is relative to the li (which is inside the padded area). The visual gap is created by the header's bottom padding + mt-4. This matches Figma closely.

designDecisions.decision: Submenu negative margin to align text with parent menu item
designDecisions.evidence: User explicitly requested: 'Make submenu item text align with menu item text by adding a negative margin with the value of the padding'. The submenu has md:p-6 (24px padding), so md:-ml-6 would shift it left by 24px to align the inner text with the parent button text.
designDecisions.recommendation: Add md:-ml-6 to the submenu container (on the div with role=menu)

designDecisions.decision: DemoContainer gets isolate class to create stacking context
designDecisions.evidence: User requested: 'Add a tailwind class on each demo container to create a new stacking context and prevent fixed behavior from happening to all blocks'. The CSS `isolation: isolate` (Tailwind class `isolate`) creates a new stacking context.
designDecisions.recommendation: Add `isolate` to DemoContainer. The first block on the test page can test fixed behavior since isolate still allows fixed positioning within the stacking context -- it just prevents z-index from bleeding between containers.

designDecisions.decision: First demo container block should have fixed enabled for testing
designDecisions.evidence: User requested: 'Make the first block fixed so that fixed behavior can be tested'. The first demo container already has fixedOnScrollDesktop=true. Note: with isolate on DemoContainer, fixed positioning within a stacking context is constrained to that context. The user may want to remove the first header from the DemoContainer entirely, or remove isolate from the first container.
designDecisions.recommendation: The first block is already fixed. However, `isolate` creates a new stacking context which means `position: fixed` inside it may not work as expected (fixed elements are positioned relative to the viewport but z-index is scoped). Actually, `isolation: isolate` only affects stacking context for z-index, it does NOT affect fixed positioning behavior. Fixed elements still position relative to the viewport. So `isolate` + `fixed` will work fine. The isolate prevents z-fighting between multiple fixed headers.

designDecisions.decision: Fix ArrowUp/ArrowDown keyboard navigation in submenu
designDecisions.evidence: User reported: 'Fix accessibility arrow navigation - ensure ArrowUp/ArrowDown actually works as expected for keyboard navigation'. Analysis of HeaderMain.js shows the keyboard handler for submenu items is only attached inside the click handler, so keyboard-opened menus (via ArrowDown on the trigger) don't get the handler.
designDecisions.recommendation: Refactor handleSubmenus() to attach keyboard handlers during initialization rather than inside click handlers. Track currentIndex based on document.activeElement position in the items list.

designDecisions.decision: Focus outline visible on keyboard navigation
designDecisions.evidence: User requested: 'Ensure accessibility-related menu item outline shows up on focused elements when browsing with keyboard'. The current styles have no explicit focus-visible outline styles on the dark background, making keyboard focus invisible.
designDecisions.recommendation: Add `focus-visible:outline focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white focus-visible:outline-offset-2` to all interactive elements (nav links, submenu buttons, submenu items, search input). The white outline will be visible against the charcoal background.

designDecisions.decision: Search placeholder uppercase, input text normal case
designDecisions.evidence: User requested: 'Search placeholder should be forced uppercase but the search input text should NOT be uppercase'. Currently the input has `uppercase` which transforms all text including user input.
designDecisions.recommendation: Replace `uppercase` with `normal-case placeholder:uppercase` on the search input elements (both desktop and mobile, both PHP and TSX).

designDecisions.evidence: User reported: 'The header isn't keeping the correct width when fixed'. When position:fixed is applied, the element is removed from document flow and loses its parent width context.
designDecisions.recommendation: Add `left-0 right-0` to the fixed state classes. The header already has px-8 padding and rounded corners, so it needs `left-0 right-0` (or a container-based approach) when fixed. Since the header has a rounded-[25px] shape, it should probably use `left-(--container-margin) right-(--container-margin)` or similar to maintain its inset appearance when fixed, matching the non-fixed state. Alternatively, use `w-full` or `inset-x-0` with appropriate margins.

questionsForUser.question: For the fixed header width correction (DEC-009): Should the fixed header span the full viewport width (left-0 right-0) or should it maintain the same visual width/margins as its non-fixed state? Currently the header is inside a container and the header-main div has rounded corners. When fixed, should it still appear inset from the viewport edges?
options: Full viewport width (left-0 right-0) -- the header stretches edge to edge when fixed
Maintain container width -- header keeps its original width constraints and is centered when fixed
questionsForUser.defaultIfNotAsked: Full viewport width with left-0 right-0, since the header-main already has px-8 padding and rounded corners that create the visual inset

questionsForUser.question: For the submenu gap (DEC-002): The current md:mt-4 (16px) creates a gap between the nav item li and the submenu dropdown. The Figma shows a small but visible gap between the bottom edge of the header's rounded container and the submenu. Should we keep mt-4, reduce to mt-2 (8px), or use a different value?
questionsForUser.defaultIfNotAsked: Keep md:mt-4 since position is relative to the li element inside the padded area, and the visual gap is created by header padding + this margin
